findings.recommendation: Do not claim three distinct project-install tiers until project and both have separate semantics, tests, and user-facing docs. Today the evidence supports two tiers: filtered consumer install and all/maintainer install.
findings.summary: Three names currently collapse into two effective primitive surfaces: project and both are equivalent; all is the maintainer/full superset.

limitations: Synthetic fixtures are not a human productivity benchmark.
Scores compare smoke outcomes and safety probes, not long-horizon quality.
Node and Go stack fixtures still need implementation before this can be called exhaustive.

Next: start coding! The AI knows what to do.

destructive_git.stderr_tail:
=== DESTRUCTIVE-GIT-BLOCKER: BLOCKED (user context) ===
BLOCKED: destructive git op 'git reset' is blocked by default (ADR-055b, r5-stash-residue).
Rationale: mutates HEAD/index/worktree state and can erase another session's staged or uncommitted work
Command: git reset --hard HEAD~1

To proceed, use ONE of:
  1. Inline flag:   append --allow-destructive to the command
                    (e.g. 'git reset --hard HEAD~1 --allow-destructive')
  2. Session env:   export COS_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE_GIT=1 (this shell only)

Reference: docs/02-Decisions/adrs/ADR-055b-destructive-git-block.md
